strings.h(3) Library Functions Manual strings.h(3)

NAME

strings.h - Centralized enum and mode string conversion API.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


const char * asciichat_mode_to_string (asciichat_mode_t mode)
Convert mode enum to string. asciichat_mode_t asciichat_string_to_mode (const char *str)
Convert string to mode enum. bool asciichat_is_valid_mode_string (const char *str)
Check if string is a valid mode name. size_t asciichat_get_all_mode_strings (const char ***out_names)
Get all mode strings. const char * asciichat_suggest_mode (const char *input)
Suggest closest matching mode using fuzzy matching. const char * color_mode_to_string (terminal_color_mode_t mode)
Convert color mode enum to string. const char * palette_type_to_string (palette_type_t type)
Convert palette type enum to string. const char * asciichat_suggest_enum_value (const char *option_name, const char *input)
Suggest closest matching enum value using fuzzy matching.

Detailed Description

Centralized enum and mode string conversion API.

Single source of truth for all enum/mode ↔ string conversions. Use these functions instead of hardcoded string literals.

Definition in file options/strings.h.

Function Documentation

size_t asciichat_get_all_mode_strings (const char *** out_names)

Get all mode strings.

Parameters

out_names Output array of mode name pointers

Returns

Number of mode names

Definition at line 85 of file options/strings.c.

85                                                                {
86   static const char *names[32]; // Max 32 modes
87   size_t count = 0;
88 
89   for (size_t i = 0; mode_registry[i].name != NULL && count < 32; i++) {
90     // Skip duplicates (like "acds" alias)
91     bool duplicate = false;
92     for (size_t j = 0; j < count; j++) {
93       if (strcmp(names[j], mode_registry[i].name) == 0) {
94         duplicate = true;
95         break;
96       }
97     }
98     if (!duplicate) {
99       names[count++] = mode_registry[i].name;
100     }
101   }
102 
103   if (out_names) {
104     *out_names = names;
105   }
106   return count;
107 }




Referenced by asciichat_suggest_mode().






bool asciichat_is_valid_mode_string (const char *
  str)


Check if string is a valid mode name.


Parameters


str String to check


Returns


true if valid mode name, false otherwise


Definition at line 81 of file options/strings.c.


81                                                      {
82   return asciichat_string_to_mode(str) != MODE_INVALID;
83 }






References asciichat_string_to_mode(), and
    MODE_INVALID.






const
  char * asciichat_mode_to_string (asciichat_mode_t mode)


Convert mode enum to string.


Parameters


mode Mode enum value


Returns


Mode name string (e.g., "server",
  "client", "mirror")


Definition at line 53 of file options/strings.c.


53                                                             {
54   // Special case: MODE_DISCOVERY is the default mode, show as "default" to users
55   if (mode == MODE_DISCOVERY) {
56     return "default";
57   }
58 
59   for (size_t i = 0; mode_registry[i].name != NULL; i++) {
60     if (mode_registry[i].mode == mode) {
61       return mode_registry[i].name;
62     }
63   }
64   return "unknown";
65 }






References MODE_DISCOVERY.






asciichat_mode_t asciichat_string_to_mode (const char *
  str)


Convert string to mode enum.


Parameters


str Mode name string


Returns


Mode enum value, or MODE_UNKNOWN if invalid


Definition at line 67 of file options/strings.c.


67                                                            {
68   if (!str) {
69     return MODE_INVALID;
70   }
71 
72   for (size_t i = 0; mode_registry[i].name != NULL; i++) {
73     if (strcmp(mode_registry[i].name, str) == 0) {
74       return mode_registry[i].mode;
75     }
76   }
77 
78   return MODE_INVALID;
79 }






References MODE_INVALID.


Referenced by asciichat_is_valid_mode_string().






const
  char * asciichat_suggest_enum_value (const char * option_name, const char *
  input)


Suggest closest matching enum value using fuzzy matching.


Parameters


option_name Option name (e.g.,
  "color-mode", "palette")


input Invalid enum value string


Returns


Suggested enum value, or NULL if no close match


Definition at line 192 of file
  options/strings.c.


192                                                                                      {
193   if (!option_name || !input) {
194     return NULL;
195   }
196 
197   size_t value_count = 0;
198   const char **values = options_get_enum_values(option_name, &value_count);
199 
200   if (!values || value_count == 0) {
201     return NULL;
202   }
203 
204   // Create NULL-terminated array for levenshtein_find_similar
205   static const char *null_terminated[64]; // Max 64 enum values
206   for (size_t i = 0; i < value_count && i < 63; i++) {
207     null_terminated[i] = values[i];
208   }
209   null_terminated[value_count] = NULL;
210 
211   return levenshtein_find_similar(input, null_terminated);
212 }






References levenshtein_find_similar(), and
    options_get_enum_values().


Referenced by parse_color_mode(),
    parse_palette_type(), and parse_render_mode().






const
  char * asciichat_suggest_mode (const char * input)


Suggest closest matching mode using fuzzy matching.


Parameters


input Invalid mode string


Returns


Suggested mode name, or NULL if no close match


Definition at line 109 of file
  options/strings.c.


109                                                       {
110   if (!input) {
111     return NULL;
112   }
113 
114   const char **names = NULL;
115   size_t count = asciichat_get_all_mode_strings(&names);
116 
117   // Create NULL-terminated array for levenshtein_find_similar
118   static const char *null_terminated[33]; // Max 32 modes + NULL
119   for (size_t i = 0; i < count && i < 32; i++) {
120     null_terminated[i] = names[i];
121   }
122   null_terminated[count] = NULL;
123 
124   return levenshtein_find_similar(input, null_terminated);
125 }






References asciichat_get_all_mode_strings(), and
    levenshtein_find_similar().






const
  char * color_mode_to_string (terminal_color_mode_t mode)


Convert color mode enum to string.


Parameters


mode Color mode value


Returns


Color mode string (e.g., "auto",
  "256", "truecolor")


Definition at line 131 of file
  options/strings.c.


131                                                              {
132   switch (mode) {
133   case TERM_COLOR_AUTO:
134     return "auto";
135   case TERM_COLOR_NONE:
136     return "none";
137   case TERM_COLOR_16:
138     return "16";
139   case TERM_COLOR_256:
140     return "256";
141   case TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR:
142     return "truecolor";
143   default:
144     return "unknown";
145   }
146 }






References TERM_COLOR_16, TERM_COLOR_256,
    TERM_COLOR_AUTO, TERM_COLOR_NONE, and
    TERM_COLOR_TRUECOLOR.


Referenced by keyboard_help_render().

170   switch (type) {
